evals.prompt: 我剛完成 BCI 憂鬱症治療的全文取得（Step 5），請幫我進行 SOTA 綜述。Session folder: Research/20260410_bci-depression-treatment/
evals.expected_output: A bilingual thematic SOTA review (step6_sota_review.md) with 4-6 themes synthesizing the 6 full-text papers on BCI-based depression treatment, plus an Obsidian Canvas knowledge graph (step6_knowledge_graph.canvas) with color-coded methodology nodes, theme groups, and labeled edges. The review should include executive summary, per-theme synthesis (consensus, debates, methods, key results), cross-theme analysis, and paper-theme mapping table.

evals.prompt: I just finished collecting full texts for my LLM-based automated program repair research. Build the SOTA review and knowledge graph. Session: Research/20260410_llm-automated-program-repair/
evals.expected_output: A bilingual thematic SOTA review with 4-6 themes covering the 11 full-text papers on LLM-APR (expect themes around prompting strategies, fine-tuning approaches, benchmark evaluations, agent-based repair, etc.). Knowledge graph canvas with CS methodology color-coding (mostly computational/orange). Cross-theme analysis should identify the rapid shift from template-based to LLM-based repair methods.

evals.prompt: 幫我用目前有的 shortlist 做 EEG ADHD 兒童的 SOTA review，我還沒跑 fulltext 但想先看看主題分析。Session: Research/20260410_eeg-adhd-children/
evals.expected_output: The skill should warn the user that no full texts are available (step5_full_text/ doesn't exist) and that synthesis will be based on abstracts only. It should still produce a SOTA review and knowledge graph using abstract data from step3_shortlist.json, but with a prominent caveat about limited synthesis depth. Bilingual output maintained.
